chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ يَشْتَرِي الرَّقِيقَ فَيَظْهَرُ بِهِ عَيْبٌ وَ مَا يُرَدُّ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يُرَدُّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 94 – The one who buys the slave so a defact is evident with him, and what is returnable from it, and what is not returnable

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/246)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/246)
text: هُ قَالَ تُرَدُّ الْجَارِيَةُ مِنْ أَرْبَعِ خِصَالٍ مِنَ الْجُنُونِ وَ الْجُذَامِ وَ الْبَرَصِ وَ الْقَرَنِ الْحَدَبَةِ إِلَّا أَنَّهَا تَكُونُ فِي الصَّدْرِ تُدْخِلُ الظَّهْرَ وَ تُخْرِجُ الصَّدْرَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Fazzal,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The slave girl would be returned from four characteristics  – from the insanity, and the leprosy, and the vitiligo, and the pair of the humps, except if she happens to be convex in the chest curving into the back and the chest coming out’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Isma’il ibn Marrar from Yunus who has narrated the following: “In the case of a man who buys a slave-girl that she is said to be a virgin but he does not find her to be as such, he (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, said, ’He (the seller) must return the difference of the price if he can verify that what he says is true.’”
